notes: Roman politician and tribune, contemporary and fellow-tribune of Caius Gracchus, known for his education, eloquence, and influence among senators.

docs.title: Life of Cato the Younger
docs.author: Plutarch
docs.excerpt: … in the government, being a very eloquent speaker, a man of the greatest temperance, and yielding in dignity to none of the Romans. It is said of Cato, that even from his infancy, in his speech, his countenance, and all his childish pastimes, he discovered an inflexible temper, unmoved
